title: running Clojure on Chez Scheme
excerpt: Dmitri's blog about programming, Clojure, and software development
content_html:
I've been working on a new Clojure implementation targeting Chez Scheme, and it's now at a point where I'd like to share it more broadly in hopes that it might be useful to others. This post will explain the rationale behind the project, its current status, and why it may be of interest.

My main goal was to make a drop-in replacement for JVM Clojure which had a fast startup and a light memory footprint. If you look under the hood of Jolt you'll quickly see why Chez Scheme is a great choice for hosting Clojure. Since both languages share the same Lisp roots, the core semantics of Clojure map seamlessly to Scheme, avoiding the impedance mismatch associated with forcing functional paradigms onto something like the JVM. Chez brings a mature JIT compiler to the table that can aggressively optimize the emitted code while maintaining a reasonably small runtime footprint. You also get a highly tuned generational garbage collector that is practically purpose built to handle the rapid allocation and deallocation of immutable data structures inherent to Clojure workflows. Finally, Chez runs natively across a wide variety of operating systems, so you end up getting lean native binaries with instant startup times rather than hauling around a heavy Java environment.

While the JVM is an incredibly powerful piece of engineering, it also happens to be one of the biggest reasons developers cite when passing on Clojure. The Java runtime evolved in an era of enterprise architecture, designed for massive application servers like Tomcat or WebSphere which were effectively their own operating systems. In that model the application server would greedily consume all available host memory and run continuously for months on end, making long JVM startup phases and heavy initial resource footprints perfectly acceptable trade-offs. But that monolithic design clashes with how modern web applications are typically built, especially in fast-paced startup environments where the engineering culture has shifted toward microservices. Today, you typically want to deploy fleets of isolated containers that boot instantly and can dynamically scale horizontally to handle sudden traffic spikes. Hauling around a massive warmed up Java environment just to run a lightweight standalone service goes directly against that principle.

I've always wondered what it would take to create a Clojure runtime that matched modern development practices, and Jolt is my attempt at tackling the problem. There are, of course, already a number of Clojure dialects out there. Jank, in particular, is an effort I'm personally excited to follow. It's already far along toward having a full Clojure implementation, but it focuses on having compatibility with the core language. Unfortunately, most of the existing Clojure libraries end up relying on some interop with the JVM host meaning that they will not run on these dialects without modification.

It would be really nice to be able to leverage the existing ecosystem of mature and battle tested libraries already available, and I was interested to see how feasible it would be to provide Java-specific shims on top of the Chez runtime. My key realization was that most libraries don't actually use much of the Java standard library surface for their interop, and once you map out a few packages like java.io, java.time, and a few others, then you can run a large portion of the current Clojure stack without having to reimplement or port it to the new platform. Again, it's worth noting other efforts such as let-go dialect targeting Go, which is following a similar plan. The key difference is in the platforms we are targeting and the ecosystems they open up access to.

What Works Today

Today, Jolt supports a number of popular Clojure libraries. It is already possible to build a fully fledged Ring app using Ring, Reitit, Selmer, and HoneySQL. The full list of libraries which have their entire test suites passing can be seen on the official documentation site. Some libraries, like Reitit, drop down to Java, but that's not a problem as Jolt makes it possible to provide shims using Jolt libraries. For example, router library implements reitit.Trie Java class needed by Reitit. So, if you need access to a particular Clojure library that doesn't have the shims available in the core language, you can always add them yourself to get them working. In many cases, this doesn't need to be done from scratch either as you can leverage mature Scheme or C libraries for the underlying functionality, and simply write a bit of glue code to expose the functionality as a Java API.

Jolt uses deps.edn for managing dependencies and aliases largely the same way as regular Clojure. As a bonus, you can also specify native C dependencies as seen here. These libraries must be provided on the system in order to be picked up.

The main joy of working with Clojure has always been its interactive development cycle, hence the familiar nREPL workflow is fully supported. You can start a Jolt app, jack in from your favorite editor, and develop it just as you would when running Clojure on the JVM. Additionally, nREPL can be embedded in compiled release binaries as well.

Another thing I wanted to do as part of the project was to map out what actually constitutes Clojure as a spec to follow. So, I'm building out a conformance spec, EBNF, and RFCs, as I add support for existing libraries and discover different quirks along the way. I'd like to give a shout out to clojure-test-suite from the Jank team which has been invaluable for getting bootstrapped and having confidence that core language semantics such as the reader, the special forms, and the bulk of clojure.core are correct. However, it stops short of the JVM host contract that many libraries depend on, so most of the effort in building the corpus went into closing that gap. It sources every expected value from reference JVM Clojure and currently holds around 3,500 cases, each tagged as either portable (:common, something any faithful Clojure dialect must satisfy) or host-dependent (:jvm, exercising interop). A certification step re-evaluates the whole corpus against real Clojure to catch unclassified divergences, ensuring that the contract doesn't quietly drift.

Under the Hood

Getting libraries to work surfaced a recurring set of problems. On the host side, Jolt mirrors nine java.* packages commonly used by popular Clojure libraries. These include java.io, java.lang, java.util, java.time, java.math, java.net, java.nio, java.sql, and java.text with roughly a thousand method and field implementations across those packages. It's worth noting that Jolt doesn't provide a full reimplementation of the JVM standard library. While most of these classes carry complete method sets, a few resolve only enough for instance? and type checks to land correctly, and a handful like bean and proxy are documented partials. In a lot of cases, it's enough that interop forms like Math/sqrt, (StringBuilder.), and instance? resolve against real behavior rather than needing to plumb out a true class hierarchy.

With no JVM underneath, class identity has to be synthesized using a hierarchy graph to back instance? and (class x), and raw Chez runtime errors need to be mapped onto a faithful JVM exception hierarchy so that catch dispatch can work correctly. The cases that plain value-equality can't catch were the trickiest to get right here. For example, the whole seq producer family has to be lazy at construction, realize left to right, memoize, and chunk in 32-element runs to match what Clojure does, while the numeric tower requires full JVM contagion rules plus 64-bit wrapping for unchecked math. The real test, though, was simply running the real test suites for individual libraries to discover subtle implementation quirks and Java host interactions. Porting spec.alpha, core.logic, core.async, test.check, tools.reader, rewrite-clj, and a few dozen others shook out most of the edge cases like data readers returning code forms, namespaced-map literals, *print-length* and *print-level*, protocol methods merging across deftype and reify, and even soft and weak references with genuine GC eviction wired through Chez's weak pairs and guardians.

In terms of footprint, a minimal binary compiles to around 13 megs without optimizations and just 8 megs when direct linked. When it comes to actual performance, Jolt is currently comparable with JVM Clojure in a number of benchmarks having parity or being around 2x slower on most, while only being around 7x worse in a couple of cases. This was made possible entirely by the mature Chez runtime. And, of course, just as we can drop down to Java with regular Clojure, Jolt lets you do the same with Scheme or even C FFI for cases where you need peak performance.

Why Not Just Wait for Leyden?

Once Project Leyden lands it will address the notorious startup times and memory footprint issues associated with the JVM, so you might rightly wonder if there is even a point in having a dedicated dialect to work around those issues anymore. I would argue that there is because we can push optimization much further by leveraging tricks like the full program optimization seen in Stalin Scheme. While both Leyden and Stalin rely on formalized closed world constraints, they have entirely different end goals. Leyden primarily focuses on shifting computation from runtime to build time to guarantee no new classes load dynamically. This approach lets AOT compilers perform aggressive dead code elimination along with devirtualization and monomorphization because knowing the full class hierarchy allows virtual method calls to become direct jumps or inlined code. But Leyden cannot achieve the same type of structural erasure that Stalin does because it must respect Java semantics. Leyden avoids heroic flow analysis, remaining bound by strict rules regarding Java object layouts and memory model. A Stalin-style optimizer, on the other hand, is entirely free to scalarize objects or bypass standard garbage collection paths when it proves an object is short lived. Unlike Leyden, it is not constrained by needing to produce a program that obeys JVM semantics and integrates with Java garbage collectors.

Building on top of Chez Scheme also sidesteps architectural baggage that Java simply cannot shed, the most obvious example being proper tail call optimization. Scheme mandates TCO by definition, so compiling Clojure down to Scheme provides infinite mutually recursive functions for free and embraces functional programming paradigms that the JVM actively fights against. The memory model and native interoperability provide another important divergence that makes the C ecosystem so appealing. Even with Leyden shrinking the JVM footprint, every value still carries the weight of a Java object header. Chez Scheme runs with much lower overhead and boots in microseconds. From Scheme you can manipulate raw C pointers and structs directly, wrapping high-performance libraries without the serialization and context-switching tax that JNI and Panama incur at the managed boundary.

There is also the reality of numeric computing with the JVM forcing dynamic types into boxed objects. As a result, there is immense garbage collection pressure during tight mathematical loops unless you rely heavily on primitive type hinting. Scheme implementations utilize techniques like NaN boxing or tagged pointers to represent dynamic types directly in CPU registers, allowing dynamic mathematical operations to run much closer to the metal. Importantly, achieving Leyden-level performance with the JVM relies on using an ahead-of-time compilation phase that destroys the interactive REPL experience that makes Clojure so powerful in the first place. Jolt can leverage Chez Scheme to generate highly optimized native machine code while retaining dynamic binding. You get the execution profile of a tightly compiled binary while keeping the interactive soul of Clojure intact. And of course, you can still do AOT to get even better performance benefits when interactive features aren't needed.

Jolt also supports tree shaking modeled on the ClojureScript compiler. When libraries are included, it's possible to trace which parts of the library are actually being used. Tracing the call graph and eliminating namespaces and functions that aren't referenced anywhere makes for a lean release binary.

Desktop and Native Interop

Aside from raw performance and memory footprint, the bigger long-term benefit comes in the form of unlocking direct access to the Scheme and C ecosystems. One obvious example here is building desktop applications. Currently, the most common approach is to use wrappers around Java UI frameworks and accept the bloat that comes from a Java runtime. Bringing Clojure to Chez Scheme sidesteps the problem, allowing you to directly bind to native C libraries like GTK through a foreign function interface. Even though Java Native Interface and even the newer Project Panama exist, they necessarily introduce friction and overhead when crossing the boundary from managed memory to native pointers. From Scheme, the C boundary becomes practically invisible, making it possible to interact with foundational native libraries without paying a runtime penalty or writing mountains of boilerplate C code just to bridge the gap.

To prove the concept, I made glimmer which uses Reagent style reactive atoms to drive GTK components. A classic TodoMVC app illustrates how using a native UI toolkit is completely seamless from Jolt. The reactive data model has proven itself to be an extremely effective tool in ClojureScript, and wrapping GTK with a Reagent style API brings these same ergonomics to a native desktop environment giving us the best of both worlds. You get to work with reactive atoms and declarative component structure while using a fast native toolkit for rendering. The application ends up having a far smaller memory footprint than anything running in a Java virtual machine possibly could. And, of course, nREPL-driven development that we all know and love works here as well.

We can even take this further and create an OpenGL context from inside a GTK window with glimmer-gl. You can see how the reactive approach works seamlessly in this context here.

I even managed to get a whole Quake style FPS working on Jolt. Building reactive 3D scenes natively usually involves manually managing state mutations in a low level language. Driving an OpenGL pipeline directly from Clojure makes it easy to model complex visual state dynamically without sacrificing raw performance. You can build local graphics intensive tools that boot quickly and run smoothly. The developer experience stays entirely within the functional world of Clojure while the heavy lifting is done by a native library.

Deployment and Architecture

Deploying or distributing a Jolt app simply means building a binary for the target platform, and users don't need to have a JVM runtime installed on their machines to run it. In fact, you don't even need Chez Scheme installed to develop using Jolt because it produces its own joltc binary that's self contained. Another advantage is that it's possible to create a Jolt library that can be embedded in C, C++, or Rust projects where Jolt can provide a high level Clojure API and interactivity via nREPL on top of performant native code. This could be particularly appealing for projects with intensive graphics visualizations or even games using engines like Godot.

Architecturally, I've structured the compiler into three parts: there's a Clojure-in-Clojure compiler for the language itself, then there's a Scheme part consisting of the host layer and Java interop shims. As a stretch goal, I'm hoping to get a portable Clojure implementation out of the deal as well that could easily target different runtimes, for cases where JVM interop isn't needed. You can start with a minimally irreducible set of forms, and then grow the compiler itself like a seed, so that the host, whether Scheme or anything else, just needs to provide support for that minimal bootstrap code. However, moving functionality into Clojure layer can also be at odds with raw performance. A compromise here might be to have a portable Scheme layer which could target different implementations.

While Chez gives us a good overall target for Clojure, it makes a few architectural assumptions, like being able to write instructions to a section of memory and then execute that code, that do not hold for targets like WASM which considers such behavior a security concern. It would be useful to decouple the host layer from Chez and make it easy to port to different Scheme runtimes which would give us access to things like interactive development for WASM via Guile. Since host code is already separate from the core language implementation, there's some more refactoring that would be needed, but it wouldn't be difficult to isolate Chez specific bits, and build a general Scheme layer on top.

On Building with LLMs

At this point, it's worth discussing the fact that this project is built using LLM tools. The reality is that it simply would not be possible for me to take on a project of this scope otherwise. Writing a full Clojure port by hand would be a full time project. Using code generation tools actually made the effort tractable, and allowed me to prove the concept quickly. Of course, there are naturally concerns associated with this approach and how I ensure code quality when I'm not writing every line of code myself. So, I'd like to take a bit of time to discuss that here.

I'm going to make the argument that whether the code was written entirely by hand or produced by an LLM is the wrong thing to focus on. To see why, we have to consider how software development actually works at scale.

There's a view that code written by hand has to be more intentional, almost has to be by definition since it requires the maintainer to actually put it in there themselves. That's, of course, true but once a project grows past a certain size or it has multiple maintainers, nobody really has the totality of the code in their heads. So, any new code that's added is always done with limited understanding. Code being written by hand should not be equated with it expressing the intent faithfully; if that were the case, then we'd never have software bugs. Humans make mistakes all the time as is clearly evidenced by there being no lack of buggy code predating LLM use.

I'm also not intimately familiar with most of the code in the projects I've been maintaining over the years. Any code I've written even a few months ago might as well have been written by someone else. When I need to make changes, I read through the code and figure out what it's doing, and I rely on the test harness to make sure I don't introduce regressions.

It's simply not feasible for humans to keep the entirety of large projects in their heads all at once. When you're working on a project, you're constantly forgetting and relearning code as you go. And the situation is even worse for projects where multiple people work together where nobody knows what everyone else was thinking. We look at the code and try to build up sufficient context in our heads to make the necessary changes. When we misjudge that context or misunderstand existing code, then we end up making mistakes.

The way we judge whether projects are actually solid is by the level of specification and testing they have, the experience of the developers, and amount of usage they see in the wild. All of these same tools work just as well with LLM generated code as they do with code written by hand.

Farming out design decisions to the LLM without reviewing the output or doing proper testing will almost certainly produce low quality code, but that is no different from somebody just slapping some code together to make a kludge rather than really thinking through a problem. Working with LLMs does not mean farming out your thinking to the machine. What these tools actually do is automate the mechanical aspect of producing the code. Once it is written, you can read it, understand it, and change it as you would with any other code.

My process tends to be to give the agent focused tasks that implement a particular feature, then make commits for each, and review to see whether a feature has been implemented as I intended. This isn't all that different from a regular code review process which is known to be a good way to catch bugs.

I also have a high level of confidence in the code because it passes clojure-test-suite along with the tests for many existing Clojure libraries. The test corpus I built up for it ensures that the code does not regress when I make changes. And benchmarks I've set up ensure that performance does not regress. All these things combined give me as much confidence as I would have in a hand written project of the same scope. I would further argue that compilers are a particularly good target for LLMs because they are well specified by definition. You have data coming in, and corresponding data coming out. There is very little ambiguity regarding what constitutes correct behavior when you are implementing a language.

Conclusion

Ultimately, the quality of the project depends on the skill and interest of its maintainer rather than how the code was produced. If somebody has been writing code for many years, and has a proven track record of delivering quality projects, then it should be assumed that when they use a new tool like an LLM in their work, the output will be consistent with the standards that they have already demonstrated previously.

If you got this far then I hope you find the project interesting and you'll give Jolt a try. Things are fairly stable at this point, and compiler binaries are available via GitHub releases and homebrew. I set up a #jolt channel on Clojurians Slack for discussion and updates, and if you're interested in collaborating or discussing the direction of the project do drop by. Alternatively, if you run into any problems or have suggestions then feel free to open up issues or make pull requests on GitHub.

This could be particularly appealing for projects with intensive graphics visualizations or even games using engines like Godot.Architecturally, I've structured the compiler into three parts: there's a Clojure-in-Clojure compiler for the language itself, then there's a Scheme part consisting of the host layer and Java interop shims. As a stretch goal, I'm hoping to get a portable Clojure implementation out of the deal as well that could easily target different runtimes, for cases where JVM interop isn't needed. You can start with a minimally irreducible set of forms, and then grow the compiler itself like a seed, so that the host, whether Scheme or anything else, just needs to provide support for that minimal bootstrap code. However, moving functionality into Clojure layer can also be at odds with raw performance. A compromise here might be to have a portable Scheme layer which could target different implementations.While Chez gives us a good overall target for Clojure, it makes a few architectural assumptions, like being able to write instructions to a section of memory and then execute that code, that do not hold for targets like WASM which considers such behavior a security concern. It would be useful to decouple the host layer from Chez and make it easy to port to different Scheme runtimes which would give us access to things like interactive development for WASM via Guile. Since host code is already separate from the core language implementation, there's some more refactoring that would be needed, but it wouldn't be difficult to isolate Chez specific bits, and build a general Scheme layer on top.On Building with LLMsAt this point, it's worth discussing the fact that this project is built using LLM tools. The reality is that it simply would not be possible for me to take on a project of this scope otherwise. Writing a full Clojure port by hand would be a full time project. Using code generation tools actually made the effort tractable, and allowed me to prove the concept quickly. Of course, there are naturally concerns associated with this approach and how I ensure code quality when I'm not writing every line of code myself. So, I'd like to take a bit of time to discuss that here.I'm going to make the argument that whether the code was written entirely by hand or produced by an LLM is the wrong thing to focus on. To see why, we have to consider how software development actually works at scale.There's a view that code written by hand has to be more intentional, almost has to be by definition since it requires the maintainer to actually put it in there themselves. That's, of course, true but once a project grows past a certain size or it has multiple maintainers, nobody really has the totality of the code in their heads. So, any new code that's added is always done with limited understanding. Code being written by hand should not be equated with it expressing the intent faithfully; if that were the case, then we'd never have software bugs. Humans make mistakes all the time as is clearly evidenced by there being no lack of buggy code predating LLM use.I'm also not intimately familiar with most of the code in the projects I've been maintaining over the years. Any code I've written even a few months ago might as well have been written by someone else. When I need to make changes, I read through the code and figure out what it's doing, and I rely on the test harness to make sure I don't introduce regressions.It's simply not feasible for humans to keep the entirety of large projects in their heads all at once. When you're working on a project, you're constantly forgetting and relearning code as you go. And the situation is even worse for projects where multiple people work together where nobody knows what everyone else was thinking. We look at the code and try to build up sufficient context in our heads to make the necessary changes. When we misjudge that context or misunderstand existing code, then we end up making mistakes.The way we judge whether projects are actually solid is by the level of specification and testing they have, the experience of the developers, and amount of usage they see in the wild. All of these same tools work just as well with LLM generated code as they do with code written by hand.Farming out design decisions to the LLM without reviewing the output or doing proper testing will almost certainly produce low quality code, but that is no different from somebody just slapping some code together to make a kludge rather than really thinking through a problem. Working with LLMs does not mean farming out your thinking to the machine. What these tools actually do is automate the mechanical aspect of producing the code. Once it is written, you can read it, understand it, and change it as you would with any other code.My process tends to be to give the agent focused tasks that implement a particular feature, then make commits for each, and review to see whether a feature has been implemented as I intended. This isn't all that different from a regular code review process which is known to be a good way to catch bugs.I also have a high level of confidence in the code because it passes clojure-test-suite along with the tests for many existing Clojure libraries. The test corpus I built up for it ensures that the code does not regress when I make changes. And benchmarks I've set up ensure that performance does not regress. All these things combined give me as much confidence as I would have in a hand written project of the same scope. I would further argue that compilers are a particularly good target for LLMs because they are well specified by definition. You have data coming in, and corresponding data coming out. There is very little ambiguity regarding what constitutes correct behavior when you are implementing a language.ConclusionUltimately, the quality of the project depends on the skill and interest of its maintainer rather than how the code was produced. If somebody has been writing code for many years, and has a proven track record of delivering quality projects, then it should be assumed that when they use a new tool like an LLM in their work, the output will be consistent with the standards that they have already demonstrated previously.If you got this far then I hope you find the project interesting and you'll give Jolt a try. Things are fairly stable at this point, and compiler binaries are available via GitHub releases and homebrew. I set up a #jolt channel on Clojurians Slack for discussion and updates, and if you're interested in collaborating or discussing the direction of the project do drop by. Alternatively, if you run into any problems or have suggestions then feel free to open up issues or make pull requests on GitHub.

blocks.html_en: I've been working on a new Clojure implementation targeting Chez Scheme, and it's now at a point where I'd like to share it more broadly in hopes that it might be useful to others. This post will explain the rationale behind the project, its current status, and why it may be of interest.

sentences.en: I've been working on a new Clojure implementation targeting Chez Scheme, and it's now at a point where I'd like to share it more broadly in hopes that it might be useful to others.
sentences.zh: 我一直在致力于针对 Chez Scheme 的新 Clojure 实现，现在我想更广泛地分享它，希望它对其他人有用。

sentences.en: This post will explain the rationale behind the project, its current status, and why it may be of interest.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章将解释该项目背后的基本原理、其当前状态以及为什么它可能会引起人们的兴趣。

blocks.html_en: My main goal was to make a drop-in replacement for JVM Clojure which had a fast startup and a light memory footprint. If you look under the hood of Jolt you'll quickly see why Chez Scheme is a great choice for hosting Clojure. Since both languages share the same Lisp roots, the core semantics of Clojure map seamlessly to Scheme, avoiding the impedance mismatch associated with forcing functional paradigms onto something like the JVM. Chez brings a mature JIT compiler to the table that can aggressively optimize the emitted code while maintaining a reasonably small runtime footprint. You also get a highly tuned generational garbage collector that is practically purpose built to handle the rapid allocation and deallocation of immutable data structures inherent to Clojure workflows. Finally, Chez runs natively across a wide variety of operating systems, so you end up getting lean native binaries with instant startup times rather than hauling around a heavy Java environment.

sentences.en: My main goal was to make a drop-in replacement for JVM Clojure which had a fast startup and a light memory footprint.
sentences.zh: 我的主要目标是制作 JVM Clojure 的直接替代品，它具有快速启动和较小的内存占用。

sentences.en: If you look under the hood of Jolt you'll quickly see why Chez Scheme is a great choice for hosting Clojure.
sentences.zh: 如果您深入了解 Jolt，您很快就会明白为什么 Chez Scheme 是托管 Clojure 的绝佳选择。

sentences.en: Since both languages share the same Lisp roots, the core semantics of Clojure map seamlessly to Scheme, avoiding the impedance mismatch associated with forcing functional paradigms onto something like the JVM.
sentences.zh: 由于两种语言共享相同的 Lisp 根源，Clojure 的核心语义无缝映射到Scheme，避免了将函数范式强加到 JVM 之类的东西上所带来的阻抗不匹配。

sentences.en: Chez brings a mature JIT compiler to the table that can aggressively optimize the emitted code while maintaining a reasonably small runtime footprint.
sentences.zh: Chez 带来了一个成熟的 JIT 编译器，可以积极优化发出的代码，同时保持相当小的运行时占用空间。

sentences.en: You also get a highly tuned generational garbage collector that is practically purpose built to handle the rapid allocation and deallocation of immutable data structures inherent to Clojure workflows.
sentences.zh: 您还可以获得高度调整的分代垃圾收集器，它实际上是专门为处理 Clojure 工作流程固有的不可变数据结构的快速分配和释放而构建的。

sentences.en: Finally, Chez runs natively across a wide variety of operating systems, so you end up getting lean native binaries with instant startup times rather than hauling around a heavy Java environment.
sentences.zh: 最后，Chez 在各种操作系统上本机运行，因此您最终会获得具有即时启动时间的精益本机二进制文件，而不是在繁重的 Java 环境中拖拽。

blocks.html_en: While the JVM is an incredibly powerful piece of engineering, it also happens to be one of the biggest reasons developers cite when passing on Clojure. The Java runtime evolved in an era of enterprise architecture, designed for massive application servers like Tomcat or WebSphere which were effectively their own operating systems. In that model the application server would greedily consume all available host memory and run continuously for months on end, making long JVM startup phases and heavy initial resource footprints perfectly acceptable trade-offs. But that monolithic design clashes with how modern web applications are typically built, especially in fast-paced startup environments where the engineering culture has shifted toward microservices. Today, you typically want to deploy fleets of isolated containers that boot instantly and can dynamically scale horizontally to handle sudden traffic spikes. Hauling around a massive warmed up Java environment just to run a lightweight standalone service goes directly against that principle.

sentences.en: While the JVM is an incredibly powerful piece of engineering, it also happens to be one of the biggest reasons developers cite when passing on Clojure.
sentences.zh: 虽然 JVM 是一项非常强大的工程，但它也恰好是开发人员放弃 Clojure 时引用的最大原因之一。

sentences.en: The Java runtime evolved in an era of enterprise architecture, designed for massive application servers like Tomcat or WebSphere which were effectively their own operating systems.
sentences.zh: Java 运行时是在企业架构时代发展起来的，专为像 Tomcat 或 WebSphere 这样的大规模应用程序服务器而设计，这些服务器实际上是它们自己的操作系统。

sentences.en: In that model the application server would greedily consume all available host memory and run continuously for months on end, making long JVM startup phases and heavy initial resource footprints perfectly acceptable trade-offs.
sentences.zh: 在该模型中，应用程序服务器会贪婪地消耗所有可用的主机内存并连续运行数月，从而使较长的 JVM 启动阶段和繁重的初始资源占用成为完全可以接受的权衡。

sentences.en: But that monolithic design clashes with how modern web applications are typically built, especially in fast-paced startup environments where the engineering culture has shifted toward microservices.
sentences.zh: 但这种整体设计与现代 Web 应用程序的通常构建方式相冲突，尤其是在工程文化已转向微服务的快节奏启动环境中。

sentences.en: Today, you typically want to deploy fleets of isolated containers that boot instantly and can dynamically scale horizontally to handle sudden traffic spikes.
sentences.zh: 如今，您通常希望部署一组独立的容器，这些容器可以立即启动，并且可以动态水平扩展以应对突然的流量峰值。

sentences.en: Hauling around a massive warmed up Java environment just to run a lightweight standalone service goes directly against that principle.
sentences.zh: 仅仅为了运行一个轻量级的独立服务而在一个巨大的预热 Java 环境中运行直接违背了这一原则。

blocks.html_en: I've always wondered what it would take to create a Clojure runtime that matched modern development practices, and Jolt is my attempt at tackling the problem. There are, of course, already a number of Clojure dialects out there. Jank, in particular, is an effort I'm personally excited to follow. It's already far along toward having a full Clojure implementation, but it focuses on having compatibility with the core language. Unfortunately, most of the existing Clojure libraries end up relying on some interop with the JVM host meaning that they will not run on these dialects without modification.

sentences.en: I've always wondered what it would take to create a Clojure runtime that matched modern development practices, and Jolt is my attempt at tackling the problem.
sentences.zh: 我一直想知道如何创建一个符合现代开发实践的 Clojure 运行时，而 Jolt 是我解决这个问题的尝试。

sentences.en: There are, of course, already a number of Clojure dialects out there.
sentences.zh: 当然，已经有许多 Clo​​jure 方言了。

sentences.en: Jank, in particular, is an effort I'm personally excited to follow.
sentences.zh: 尤其是 Jank，我个人很高兴能够关注这一成果。

sentences.en: It's already far along toward having a full Clojure implementation, but it focuses on having compatibility with the core language.
sentences.zh: 距离完整的 Clojure 实现已经很远了，但它的重点是与核心语言的兼容性。

sentences.en: Unfortunately, most of the existing Clojure libraries end up relying on some interop with the JVM host meaning that they will not run on these dialects without modification.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，大多数现有的 Clojure 库最终都依赖于与 JVM 主机的某些互操作，这意味着它们在不进行修改的情况下将无法在这些方言上运行。

blocks.html_en: It would be really nice to be able to leverage the existing ecosystem of mature and battle tested libraries already available, and I was interested to see how feasible it would be to provide Java-specific shims on top of the Chez runtime. My key realization was that most libraries don't actually use much of the Java standard library surface for their interop, and once you map out a few packages like java.io, java.time, and a few others, then you can run a large portion of the current Clojure stack without having to reimplement or port it to the new platform. Again, it's worth noting other efforts such as let-go dialect targeting Go, which is following a similar plan. The key difference is in the platforms we are targeting and the ecosystems they open up access to.

sentences.en: It would be really nice to be able to leverage the existing ecosystem of mature and battle tested libraries already available, and I was interested to see how feasible it would be to provide Java-specific shims on top of the Chez runtime.
sentences.zh: 如果能够利用现有的成熟且经过实战测试的库组成的生态系统，那就太好了，而且我很想知道在 Chez 运行时之上提供特定于 Java 的填充程序有多么可行。

sentences.en: My key realization was that most libraries don't actually use much of the Java standard library surface for their interop, and once you map out a few packages like java.io, java.time, and a few others, then you can run a large portion of the current Clojure stack without having to reimplement or port it to the new platform.
sentences.zh: 我的主要认识是，大多数库实际上并没有使用太多 Java 标准库表面来进行互操作，一旦您映射了一些包，例如 java.io、java.time 和其他一些包，那么您就可以运行当前 Clojure 堆栈的大部分，而无需重新实现或将其移植到新平台。

sentences.en: Again, it's worth noting other efforts such as let-go dialect targeting Go, which is following a similar plan.
sentences.zh: 同样值得注意的是其他努力，例如针对 Go 的放手方言，它正在遵循类似的计划。

sentences.en: The key difference is in the platforms we are targeting and the ecosystems they open up access to.
sentences.zh: 主要区别在于我们的目标平台以及它们开放的生态系统。

blocks.html_en: What Works Today

sentences.en: What Works Today
sentences.zh: 今天有效的方法

blocks.html_en: Today, Jolt supports a number of popular Clojure libraries. It is already possible to build a fully fledged Ring app using Ring, Reitit, Selmer, and HoneySQL. The full list of libraries which have their entire test suites passing can be seen on the official documentation site. Some libraries, like Reitit, drop down to Java, but that's not a problem as Jolt makes it possible to provide shims using Jolt libraries. For example, router library implements reitit.Trie Java class needed by Reitit. So, if you need access to a particular Clojure library that doesn't have the shims available in the core language, you can always add them yourself to get them working. In many cases, this doesn't need to be done from scratch either as you can leverage mature Scheme or C libraries for the underlying functionality, and simply write a bit of glue code to expose the functionality as a Java API.

sentences.zh: 如今，Jolt 支持许多流行的 Clojure 库。

sentences.en: In many cases, this doesn't need to be done from scratch either as you can leverage mature Scheme or C libraries for the underlying functionality, and simply write a bit of glue code to expose the functionality as a Java API.
sentences.zh: 在许多情况下，这不需要从头开始完成，因为您可以利用成熟的Scheme 或C 库来实现底层功能，并且只需编写一些粘合代码即可将功能公开为Java API。

blocks.html_en: Jolt uses deps.edn for managing dependencies and aliases largely the same way as regular Clojure. As a bonus, you can also specify native C dependencies as seen here. These libraries must be provided on the system in order to be picked up.

sentences.zh: Jolt 使用 deps.edn 来管理依赖项和别名，其方式与常规 Clojure 基本相同。

sentences.en: As a bonus, you can also specify native C dependencies as seen here.
sentences.zh: 作为奖励，您还可以指定本机 C 依赖项，如此处所示。

sentences.en: These libraries must be provided on the system in order to be picked up.
sentences.zh: 系统上必须提供这些库才能使用。

blocks.html_en: The main joy of working with Clojure has always been its interactive development cycle, hence the familiar nREPL workflow is fully supported. You can start a Jolt app, jack in from your favorite editor, and develop it just as you would when running Clojure on the JVM. Additionally, nREPL can be embedded in compiled release binaries as well.

sentences.en: The main joy of working with Clojure has always been its interactive development cycle, hence the familiar nREPL workflow is fully supported.
sentences.zh: 使用 Clojure 的主要乐趣始终是其交互式开发周期，因此完全支持熟悉的 nREPL 工作流程。

sentences.en: You can start a Jolt app, jack in from your favorite editor, and develop it just as you would when running Clojure on the JVM.
sentences.zh: 您可以启动 Jolt 应用程序，从您最喜欢的编辑器中插入，然后像在 JVM 上运行 Clojure 时一样开发它。

sentences.en: Additionally, nREPL can be embedded in compiled release binaries as well.
sentences.zh: 此外，nREPL 也可以嵌入到已编译的发行二进制文件中。

blocks.html_en: Another thing I wanted to do as part of the project was to map out what actually constitutes Clojure as a spec to follow. So, I'm building out a conformance spec, EBNF, and RFCs, as I add support for existing libraries and discover different quirks along the way. I'd like to give a shout out to clojure-test-suite from the Jank team which has been invaluable for getting bootstrapped and having confidence that core language semantics such as the reader, the special forms, and the bulk of clojure.core are correct. However, it stops short of the JVM host contract that many libraries depend on, so most of the effort in building the corpus went into closing that gap. It sources every expected value from reference JVM Clojure and currently holds around 3,500 cases, each tagged as either portable (:common, something any faithful Clojure dialect must satisfy) or host-dependent (:jvm, exercising interop). A certification step re-evaluates the whole corpus against real Clojure to catch unclassified divergences, ensuring that the contract doesn't quietly drift.

sentences.zh: 作为该项目的一部分，我想做的另一件事是找出 Clojure 的实际构成，作为要遵循的规范。

sentences.zh: 因此，我正在构建一致性规范、EBNF 和 RFC，同时添加对现有库的支持并在此过程中发现不同的怪癖。

sentences.en: I'd like to give a shout out to clojure-test-suite from the Jank team which has been invaluable for getting bootstrapped and having confidence that core language semantics such as the reader, the special forms, and the bulk of clojure.core are correct.
sentences.zh: 我想对 Jank 团队的 clojure-test-suite 表示赞扬，它对于引导和相信核心语言语义（例如阅读器、特殊形式和大部分 clojure.core 是正确的）非常宝贵。

sentences.en: However, it stops short of the JVM host contract that many libraries depend on, so most of the effort in building the corpus went into closing that gap.
sentences.zh: 然而，它缺乏许多库所依赖的 JVM 主机契约，因此构建语料库的大部分努力都花在了缩小这一差距上。

sentences.en: It sources every expected value from reference JVM Clojure and currently holds around 3,500 cases, each tagged as either portable (:common, something any faithful Clojure dialect must satisfy) or host-dependent (:jvm, exercising interop).
sentences.zh: 它从参考 JVM Clojure 中获取每个预期值，目前保存了大约 3,500 个案例，每个案例都标记为可移植（：common，任何忠实的 Clojure 方言都必须满足的要求）或依赖于主机（：jvm，执行互操作）。

sentences.zh: 认证步骤会根据真实的 Clojure 重新评估整个语料库，以捕获未分类的差异，确保合约不会悄然发生变化。

blocks.html_en: Under the Hood

sentences.en: Under the Hood
sentences.zh: 引擎盖下

blocks.html_en: Getting libraries to work surfaced a recurring set of problems. On the host side, Jolt mirrors nine java.* packages commonly used by popular Clojure libraries. These include java.io, java.lang, java.util, java.time, java.math, java.net, java.nio, java.sql, and java.text with roughly a thousand method and field implementations across those packages. It's worth noting that Jolt doesn't provide a full reimplementation of the JVM standard library. While most of these classes carry complete method sets, a few resolve only enough for instance? and type checks to land correctly, and a handful like bean and proxy are documented partials. In a lot of cases, it's enough that interop forms like Math/sqrt, (StringBuilder.), and instance? resolve against real behavior rather than needing to plumb out a true class hierarchy.

sentences.en: Getting libraries to work surfaced a recurring set of problems.
sentences.zh: 让图书馆正常工作暴露了一系列反复出现的问题。

sentences.en: On the host side, Jolt mirrors nine java.* packages commonly used by popular Clojure libraries.

blocks.html_en: With no JVM underneath, class identity has to be synthesized using a hierarchy graph to back instance? and (class x), and raw Chez runtime errors need to be mapped onto a faithful JVM exception hierarchy so that catch dispatch can work correctly. The cases that plain value-equality can't catch were the trickiest to get right here. For example, the whole seq producer family has to be lazy at construction, realize left to right, memoize, and chunk in 32-element runs to match what Clojure does, while the numeric tower requires full JVM contagion rules plus 64-bit wrapping for unchecked math. The real test, though, was simply running the real test suites for individual libraries to discover subtle implementation quirks and Java host interactions. Porting spec.alpha, core.logic, core.async, test.check, tools.reader, rewrite-clj, and a few dozen others shook out most of the edge cases like data readers returning code forms, namespaced-map literals, *print-length* and *print-level*, protocol methods merging across deftype and reify, and even soft and weak references with genuine GC eviction wired through Chez's weak pairs and guardians.

blocks.html_en: In terms of footprint, a minimal binary compiles to around 13 megs without optimizations and just 8 megs when direct linked. blocks.html_en: Why Not Just Wait for Leyden?

sentences.en: Why Not Just Wait for Leyden?
sentences.zh: 为什么不等莱顿呢？

blocks.html_en: Once Project Leyden lands it will address the notorious startup times and memory footprint issues associated with the JVM, so you might rightly wonder if there is even a point in having a dedicated dialect to work around those issues anymore. I would argue that there is because we can push optimization much further by leveraging tricks like the full program optimization seen in Stalin Scheme. While both Leyden and Stalin rely on formalized closed world constraints, they have entirely different end goals. Leyden primarily focuses on shifting computation from runtime to build time to guarantee no new classes load dynamically. This approach lets AOT compilers perform aggressive dead code elimination along with devirtualization and monomorphization because knowing the full class hierarchy allows virtual method calls to become direct jumps or inlined code. But Leyden cannot achieve the same type of structural erasure that Stalin does because it must respect Java semantics. Leyden avoids heroic flow analysis, remaining bound by strict rules regarding Java object layouts and memory model. A Stalin-style optimizer, on the other hand, is entirely free to scalarize objects or bypass standard garbage collection paths when it proves an object is short lived. Unlike Leyden, it is not constrained by needing to produce a program that obeys JVM semantics and integrates with Java garbage collectors.

sentences.zh: 另一方面，斯大林式优化器在证明对象寿命较短时，可以完全自由地缩放对象或绕过标准垃圾收集路径。

sentences.en: Unlike Leyden, it is not constrained by needing to produce a program that obeys JVM semantics and integrates with Java garbage collectors.

blocks.html_en: Building on top of Chez Scheme also sidesteps architectural baggage that Java simply cannot shed, the most obvious example being proper tail call optimization. Scheme mandates TCO by definition, so compiling Clojure down to Scheme provides infinite mutually recursive functions for free and embraces functional programming paradigms that the JVM actively fights against. The memory model and native interoperability provide another important divergence that makes the C ecosystem so appealing. Even with Leyden shrinking the JVM footprint, every value still carries the weight of a Java object header. Chez Scheme runs with much lower overhead and boots in microseconds. From Scheme you can manipulate raw C pointers and structs directly, wrapping high-performance libraries without the serialization and context-switching tax that JNI and Panama incur at the managed boundary.

sentences.en: Building on top of Chez Scheme also sidesteps architectural baggage that Java simply cannot shed, the most obvious example being proper tail call optimization.
sentences.zh: 在 Chez Scheme 之上构建还回避了 Java 根本无法摆脱的架构包袱，最明显的例子是适当的尾调用优化。

sentences.zh: 内存模型和本机互操作性提供了另一个重要的差异，使 C 生态系统如此有吸引力。

sentences.zh: 从Scheme中，您可以直接操作原始C指针和结构，包装高性能库，而无需JNI和Panama在托管边界产生序列化和上下文切换税。

blocks.html_en: There is also the reality of numeric computing with the JVM forcing dynamic types into boxed objects. As a result, there is immense garbage collection pressure during tight mathematical loops unless you rely heavily on primitive type hinting. Scheme implementations utilize techniques like NaN boxing or tagged pointers to represent dynamic types directly in CPU registers, allowing dynamic mathematical operations to run much closer to the metal. Importantly, achieving Leyden-level performance with the JVM relies on using an ahead-of-time compilation phase that destroys the interactive REPL experience that makes Clojure so powerful in the first place. Jolt can leverage Chez Scheme to generate highly optimized native machine code while retaining dynamic binding. You get the execution profile of a tightly compiled binary while keeping the interactive soul of Clojure intact. And of course, you can still do AOT to get even better performance benefits when interactive features aren't needed.

sentences.en: There is also the reality of numeric computing with the JVM forcing dynamic types into boxed objects.
sentences.zh: 还有一个现实是数值计算，JVM 强制将动态类型放入装箱对象中。

blocks.html_en: Jolt also supports tree shaking modeled on the ClojureScript compiler. When libraries are included, it's possible to trace which parts of the library are actually being used. Tracing the call graph and eliminating namespaces and functions that aren't referenced anywhere makes for a lean release binary.

blocks.html_en: Desktop and Native Interop

sentences.en: Desktop and Native Interop
sentences.zh: 桌面和本机互操作

blocks.html_en: Aside from raw performance and memory footprint, the bigger long-term benefit comes in the form of unlocking direct access to the Scheme and C ecosystems. One obvious example here is building desktop applications. Currently, the most common approach is to use wrappers around Java UI frameworks and accept the bloat that comes from a Java runtime. Bringing Clojure to Chez Scheme sidesteps the problem, allowing you to directly bind to native C libraries like GTK through a foreign function interface. Even though Java Native Interface and even the newer Project Panama exist, they necessarily introduce friction and overhead when crossing the boundary from managed memory to native pointers. From Scheme, the C boundary becomes practically invisible, making it possible to interact with foundational native libraries without paying a runtime penalty or writing mountains of boilerplate C code just to bridge the gap.

sentences.zh: 除了原始性能和内存占用之外，更大的长期好处还在于可以直接访问Scheme 和C 生态系统。

blocks.html_en: To prove the concept, I made glimmer which uses Reagent style reactive atoms to drive GTK components. A classic TodoMVC app illustrates how using a native UI toolkit is completely seamless from Jolt. The reactive data model has proven itself to be an extremely effective tool in ClojureScript, and wrapping GTK with a Reagent style API brings these same ergonomics to a native desktop environment giving us the best of both worlds. You get to work with reactive atoms and declarative component structure while using a fast native toolkit for rendering. The application ends up having a far smaller memory footprint than anything running in a Java virtual machine possibly could. And, of course, nREPL-driven development that we all know and love works here as well.

sentences.zh: 为了证明这个概念，我制作了 glimmer，它使用 Reagent 风格的反应原子来驱动 GTK 组件。

blocks.html_en: We can even take this further and create an OpenGL context from inside a GTK window with glimmer-gl. You can see how the reactive approach works seamlessly in this context here.

sentences.zh: 我们甚至可以更进一步，使用 glimmer-gl 从 GTK 窗口内部创建 OpenGL 上下文。

blocks.html_en: I even managed to get a whole Quake style FPS working on Jolt. Building reactive 3D scenes natively usually involves manually managing state mutations in a low level language. Driving an OpenGL pipeline directly from Clojure makes it easy to model complex visual state dynamically without sacrificing raw performance. You can build local graphics intensive tools that boot quickly and run smoothly. The developer experience stays entirely within the functional world of Clojure while the heavy lifting is done by a native library.

blocks.html_en: Deployment and Architecture

sentences.en: Deployment and Architecture
sentences.zh: 部署与架构

blocks.html_en: Deploying or distributing a Jolt app simply means building a binary for the target platform, and users don't need to have a JVM runtime installed on their machines to run it. In fact, you don't even need Chez Scheme installed to develop using Jolt because it produces its own joltc binary that's self contained. Another advantage is that it's possible to create a Jolt library that can be embedded in C, C++, or Rust projects where Jolt can provide a high level Clojure API and interactivity via nREPL on top of performant native code. This could be particularly appealing for projects with intensive graphics visualizations or even games using engines like Godot.

sentences.en: Deploying or distributing a Jolt app simply means building a binary for the target platform, and users don't need to have a JVM runtime installed on their machines to run it.
sentences.zh: 部署或分发 Jolt 应用程序仅意味着为目标平台构建二进制文件，用户不需要在其计算机上安装 JVM 运行时即可运行它。

sentences.en: In fact, you don't even need Chez Scheme installed to develop using Jolt because it produces its own joltc binary that's self contained.
sentences.zh: 事实上，您甚至不需要安装 Chez Scheme 来使用 Jolt 进行开发，因为它会生成自己的独立的 joltc 二进制文件。

sentences.en: Another advantage is that it's possible to create a Jolt library that can be embedded in C, C++, or Rust projects where Jolt can provide a high level Clojure API and interactivity via nREPL on top of performant native code.
sentences.zh: 另一个优点是可以创建一个可嵌入到 C、C++ 或 Rust 项目中的 Jolt 库，其中 Jolt 可以在高性能本机代码之上通过 nREPL 提供高级 Clojure API 和交互性。

sentences.en: This could be particularly appealing for projects with intensive graphics visualizations or even games using engines like Godot.
sentences.zh: 这对于具有密集图形可视化的项目甚至使用 Godot 等引擎的游戏尤其有吸引力。

blocks.html_en: Architecturally, I've structured the compiler into three parts: there's a Clojure-in-Clojure compiler for the language itself, then there's a Scheme part consisting of the host layer and Java interop shims. As a stretch goal, I'm hoping to get a portable Clojure implementation out of the deal as well that could easily target different runtimes, for cases where JVM interop isn't needed. You can start with a minimally irreducible set of forms, and then grow the compiler itself like a seed, so that the host, whether Scheme or anything else, just needs to provide support for that minimal bootstrap code. However, moving functionality into Clojure layer can also be at odds with raw performance. A compromise here might be to have a portable Scheme layer which could target different implementations.

sentences.en: Architecturally, I've structured the compiler into three parts: there's a Clojure-in-Clojure compiler for the language itself, then there's a Scheme part consisting of the host layer and Java interop shims.
sentences.zh: 在架构上，我将编译器分为三个部分：语言本身有一个 Clojure-in-Clojure 编译器，然后是一个由主机层和 Java 互操作垫片组成的方案部分。

sentences.zh: 作为一个延伸目标，我希望能够从交易中获得一个可移植的 Clojure 实现，它可以轻松地针对不同的运行时，以应对不需要 JVM 互操作的情况。

sentences.en: You can start with a minimally irreducible set of forms, and then grow the compiler itself like a seed, so that the host, whether Scheme or anything else, just needs to provide support for that minimal bootstrap code.
sentences.zh: 您可以从一组最小不可约形式开始，然后像种子一样生长编译器本身，以便主机（无论是Scheme还是其他任何东西）只需要为该最小引导代码提供支持。

sentences.en: However, moving functionality into Clojure layer can also be at odds with raw performance.
sentences.zh: 然而，将功能转移到 Clojure 层也可能与原始性能不一致。

sentences.en: A compromise here might be to have a portable Scheme layer which could target different implementations.
sentences.zh: 这里的一个折衷方案可能是拥有一个可移植的方案层，它可以针对不同的实现。

blocks.html_en: While Chez gives us a good overall target for Clojure, it makes a few architectural assumptions, like being able to write instructions to a section of memory and then execute that code, that do not hold for targets like WASM which considers such behavior a security concern. It would be useful to decouple the host layer from Chez and make it easy to port to different Scheme runtimes which would give us access to things like interactive development for WASM via Guile. Since host code is already separate from the core language implementation, there's some more refactoring that would be needed, but it wouldn't be difficult to isolate Chez specific bits, and build a general Scheme layer on top.

sentences.en: While Chez gives us a good overall target for Clojure, it makes a few architectural assumptions, like being able to write instructions to a section of memory and then execute that code, that do not hold for targets like WASM which considers such behavior a security concern.
sentences.zh: 虽然 Chez 为我们提供了 Clojure 的良好总体目标，但它做出了一些架构假设，例如能够将指令写入内存的一部分，然后执行该代码，这不适用于 WASM 等目标，因为 WASM 认为这种行为是安全问题。

sentences.en: It would be useful to decouple the host layer from Chez and make it easy to port to different Scheme runtimes which would give us access to things like interactive development for WASM via Guile.
sentences.zh: 将主机层与 Chez 解耦并使其轻松移植到不同的 Scheme 运行时将非常有用，这将使我们能够通过 Guile 访问 WASM 的交互式开发等内容。

sentences.en: Since host code is already separate from the core language implementation, there's some more refactoring that would be needed, but it wouldn't be difficult to isolate Chez specific bits, and build a general Scheme layer on top.
sentences.zh: 由于主机代码已经与核心语言实现分离，因此需要进行更多重构，但隔离 Chez 特定位并在顶部构建通用方案层并不困难。

blocks.html_en: On Building with LLMs

sentences.en: On Building with LLMs
sentences.zh: 论法学硕士的建设

blocks.html_en: At this point, it's worth discussing the fact that this project is built using LLM tools. The reality is that it simply would not be possible for me to take on a project of this scope otherwise. Writing a full Clojure port by hand would be a full time project. Using code generation tools actually made the effort tractable, and allowed me to prove the concept quickly. Of course, there are naturally concerns associated with this approach and how I ensure code quality when I'm not writing every line of code myself. So, I'd like to take a bit of time to discuss that here.

sentences.en: At this point, it's worth discussing the fact that this project is built using LLM tools.
sentences.zh: 在这一点上，值得讨论的是这个项目是使用 LLM 工具构建的。

sentences.en: The reality is that it simply would not be possible for me to take on a project of this scope otherwise.
sentences.zh: 现实情况是，否则我根本不可能承担如此规模的项目。

sentences.en: Writing a full Clojure port by hand would be a full time project.
sentences.zh: 手动编写完整的 Clojure 移植将是一个全职项目。

sentences.en: Using code generation tools actually made the effort tractable, and allowed me to prove the concept quickly.
sentences.zh: 使用代码生成工具实际上使工作变得容易处理，并让我能够快速证明这个概念。

sentences.en: Of course, there are naturally concerns associated with this approach and how I ensure code quality when I'm not writing every line of code myself.
sentences.zh: 当然，自然会有与这种方法相关的担忧，以及当我不是自己编写每一行代码时如何确保代码质量。

sentences.en: So, I'd like to take a bit of time to discuss that here.
sentences.zh: 所以，我想花一点时间在这里讨论这个问题。

blocks.html_en: I'm going to make the argument that whether the code was written entirely by hand or produced by an LLM is the wrong thing to focus on. To see why, we have to consider how software development actually works at scale.

sentences.en: I'm going to make the argument that whether the code was written entirely by hand or produced by an LLM is the wrong thing to focus on.
sentences.zh: 我要提出的论点是，代码是否完全由手工编写或由法学硕士生成是错误的关注点。

sentences.en: To see why, we have to consider how software development actually works at scale.
sentences.zh: 要了解其中的原因，我们必须考虑软件开发实际上是如何大规模运作的。

blocks.html_en: There's a view that code written by hand has to be more intentional, almost has to be by definition since it requires the maintainer to actually put it in there themselves. That's, of course, true but once a project grows past a certain size or it has multiple maintainers, nobody really has the totality of the code in their heads. So, any new code that's added is always done with limited understanding. Code being written by hand should not be equated with it expressing the intent faithfully; if that were the case, then we'd never have software bugs. Humans make mistakes all the time as is clearly evidenced by there being no lack of buggy code predating LLM use.

sentences.en: There's a view that code written by hand has to be more intentional, almost has to be by definition since it requires the maintainer to actually put it in there themselves.
sentences.zh: 有一种观点认为，手工编写的代码必须更加有意，几乎必须是根据定义，因为它需要维护者自己实际将其放入其中。

sentences.en: That's, of course, true but once a project grows past a certain size or it has multiple maintainers, nobody really has the totality of the code in their heads.
sentences.zh: 当然，这是事实，但一旦项目规模超过一定规模或有多个维护者，没有人真正掌握完整的代码。

sentences.en: So, any new code that's added is always done with limited understanding.
sentences.zh: 因此，添加的任何新代码总是在理解有限的情况下完成的。

sentences.en: Code being written by hand should not be equated with it expressing the intent faithfully; if that were the case, then we'd never have software bugs.
sentences.zh: 手写的代码不应该等同于忠实地表达了意图；如果是这样的话，那么我们就永远不会出现软件错误。

sentences.en: Humans make mistakes all the time as is clearly evidenced by there being no lack of buggy code predating LLM use.
sentences.zh: 人类总是会犯错误，在 LLM 使用之前就不乏有错误的代码就清楚地证明了这一点。

blocks.html_en: I'm also not intimately familiar with most of the code in the projects I've been maintaining over the years. Any code I've written even a few months ago might as well have been written by someone else. When I need to make changes, I read through the code and figure out what it's doing, and I rely on the test harness to make sure I don't introduce regressions.

sentences.en: I'm also not intimately familiar with most of the code in the projects I've been maintaining over the years.
sentences.zh: 我对多年来一直维护的项目中的大部分代码也不太熟悉。

sentences.en: Any code I've written even a few months ago might as well have been written by someone else.
sentences.zh: 我几个月前编写的任何代码也可能是其他人编写的。

sentences.en: When I need to make changes, I read through the code and figure out what it's doing, and I rely on the test harness to make sure I don't introduce regressions.
sentences.zh: 当我需要进行更改时，我会通读代码并弄清楚它在做什么，并且我依靠测试工具来确保我不会引入回归。

blocks.html_en: It's simply not feasible for humans to keep the entirety of large projects in their heads all at once. When you're working on a project, you're constantly forgetting and relearning code as you go. And the situation is even worse for projects where multiple people work together where nobody knows what everyone else was thinking. We look at the code and try to build up sufficient context in our heads to make the necessary changes. When we misjudge that context or misunderstand existing code, then we end up making mistakes.

sentences.en: It's simply not feasible for humans to keep the entirety of large projects in their heads all at once.
sentences.zh: 对于人类来说，一次性将所有大型项目都记在脑子里是不可能的。

sentences.en: When you're working on a project, you're constantly forgetting and relearning code as you go.
sentences.zh: 当您从事一个项目时，您会不断忘记并重新学习代码。

sentences.en: And the situation is even worse for projects where multiple people work together where nobody knows what everyone else was thinking.
sentences.zh: 对于多人一起工作、没有人知道其他人在想什么的项目来说，情况更糟。

sentences.en: We look at the code and try to build up sufficient context in our heads to make the necessary changes.
sentences.zh: 我们查看代码并尝试在头脑中建立足够的上下文以进行必要的更改。

sentences.en: When we misjudge that context or misunderstand existing code, then we end up making mistakes.
sentences.zh: 当我们错误判断上下文或误解现有代码时，我们最终会犯错误。

blocks.html_en: The way we judge whether projects are actually solid is by the level of specification and testing they have, the experience of the developers, and amount of usage they see in the wild. All of these same tools work just as well with LLM generated code as they do with code written by hand.

sentences.en: The way we judge whether projects are actually solid is by the level of specification and testing they have, the experience of the developers, and amount of usage they see in the wild.
sentences.zh: 我们判断项目是否真正可靠的方法是根据它们的规范和测试水平、开发人员的经验以及他们在野外看到的使用量。

sentences.en: All of these same tools work just as well with LLM generated code as they do with code written by hand.
sentences.zh: 所有这些相同的工具都可以很好地处理 LLM 生成的代码，就像处理手工编写的代码一样。

blocks.html_en: Farming out design decisions to the LLM without reviewing the output or doing proper testing will almost certainly produce low quality code, but that is no different from somebody just slapping some code together to make a kludge rather than really thinking through a problem. Working with LLMs does not mean farming out your thinking to the machine. What these tools actually do is automate the mechanical aspect of producing the code. Once it is written, you can read it, understand it, and change it as you would with any other code.

sentences.en: Farming out design decisions to the LLM without reviewing the output or doing proper testing will almost certainly produce low quality code, but that is no different from somebody just slapping some code together to make a kludge rather than really thinking through a problem.
sentences.zh: 将设计决策外包给法学硕士而不审查输出或进行适当的测试几乎肯定会产生低质量的代码，但这与有人只是将一些代码拼凑在一起而不是真正思考问题没有什么不同。

sentences.en: Working with LLMs does not mean farming out your thinking to the machine.
sentences.zh: 与法学硕士合作并不意味着将你的思维外包给机器。

sentences.en: What these tools actually do is automate the mechanical aspect of producing the code.
sentences.zh: 这些工具实际上所做的是自动化生成代码的机械方面。

sentences.en: Once it is written, you can read it, understand it, and change it as you would with any other code.
sentences.zh: 一旦编写完成，您就可以阅读、理解并更改它，就像处理任何其他代码一样。

blocks.html_en: My process tends to be to give the agent focused tasks that implement a particular feature, then make commits for each, and review to see whether a feature has been implemented as I intended. This isn't all that different from a regular code review process which is known to be a good way to catch bugs.

sentences.en: My process tends to be to give the agent focused tasks that implement a particular feature, then make commits for each, and review to see whether a feature has been implemented as I intended.
sentences.zh: 我的流程往往是为代理提供实现特定功能的重点任务，然后对每个功能进行提交，并检查功能是否已按我的预期实现。

sentences.en: This isn't all that different from a regular code review process which is known to be a good way to catch bugs.
sentences.zh: 这与常规代码审查过程并没有什么不同，众所周知，常规代码审查过程是捕获错误的好方法。

blocks.html_en: I also have a high level of confidence in the code because it passes clojure-test-suite along with the tests for many existing Clojure libraries. The test corpus I built up for it ensures that the code does not regress when I make changes. And benchmarks I've set up ensure that performance does not regress. All these things combined give me as much confidence as I would have in a hand written project of the same scope. I would further argue that compilers are a particularly good target for LLMs because they are well specified by definition. You have data coming in, and corresponding data coming out. There is very little ambiguity regarding what constitutes correct behavior when you are implementing a language.

sentences.en: I also have a high level of confidence in the code because it passes clojure-test-suite along with the tests for many existing Clojure libraries.
sentences.zh: 我对代码也非常有信心，因为它通过了 clojure-test-suite 以及许多现有 Clojure 库的测试。

sentences.en: The test corpus I built up for it ensures that the code does not regress when I make changes.
sentences.zh: 我为其构建的测试语料库可确保当我进行更改时代码不会回归。

sentences.en: And benchmarks I've set up ensure that performance does not regress.
sentences.zh: 我设置的基准可确保性能不会下降。

sentences.en: All these things combined give me as much confidence as I would have in a hand written project of the same scope.
sentences.zh: 所有这些事情加在一起给了我与我对相同范围的手写项目一样的信心。

sentences.en: I would further argue that compilers are a particularly good target for LLMs because they are well specified by definition.
sentences.zh: 我进一步认为，编译器是法学硕士特别好的目标，因为它们的定义很好地指定了。

sentences.en: You have data coming in, and corresponding data coming out.
sentences.zh: 你有数据进来，相应的数据也出来。

sentences.en: There is very little ambiguity regarding what constitutes correct behavior when you are implementing a language.
sentences.zh: 当您实现一种语言时，关于什么构成正确行为几乎没有什么歧义。

blocks.html_en: Conclusion

sentences.en: Conclusion
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: Ultimately, the quality of the project depends on the skill and interest of its maintainer rather than how the code was produced. If somebody has been writing code for many years, and has a proven track record of delivering quality projects, then it should be assumed that when they use a new tool like an LLM in their work, the output will be consistent with the standards that they have already demonstrated previously.

sentences.en: Ultimately, the quality of the project depends on the skill and interest of its maintainer rather than how the code was produced.
sentences.zh: 最终，项目的质量取决于其维护者的技能和兴趣，而不是代码的生成方式。

sentences.en: If somebody has been writing code for many years, and has a proven track record of delivering quality projects, then it should be assumed that when they use a new tool like an LLM in their work, the output will be consistent with the standards that they have already demonstrated previously.
sentences.zh: 如果某人已经编写代码多年，并且在交付高质量项目方面拥有良好的记录，那么应该假设，当他们在工作中使用 LLM 这样的新工具时，输出将与他们之前已经展示的标准一致。

content_html_zh:
我一直在致力于针对 Chez Scheme 的新 Clojure 实现，现在我想更广泛地分享它，希望它对其他人有用。 这篇文章将解释该项目背后的基本原理、其当前状态以及为什么它可能会引起人们的兴趣。

我的主要目标是制作 JVM Clojure 的直接替代品，它具有快速启动和较小的内存占用。 如果您深入了解 Jolt，您很快就会明白为什么 Chez Scheme 是托管 Clojure 的绝佳选择。 由于两种语言共享相同的 Lisp 根源，Clojure 的核心语义无缝映射到Scheme，避免了将函数范式强加到 JVM 之类的东西上所带来的阻抗不匹配。 Chez 带来了一个成熟的 JIT 编译器，可以积极优化发出的代码，同时保持相当小的运行时占用空间。 您还可以获得高度调整的分代垃圾收集器，它实际上是专门为处理 Clojure 工作流程固有的不可变数据结构的快速分配和释放而构建的。 最后，Chez 在各种操作系统上本机运行，因此您最终会获得具有即时启动时间的精益本机二进制文件，而不是在繁重的 Java 环境中拖拽。

虽然 JVM 是一项非常强大的工程，但它也恰好是开发人员放弃 Clojure 时引用的最大原因之一。 Java 运行时是在企业架构时代发展起来的，专为像 Tomcat 或 WebSphere 这样的大规模应用程序服务器而设计，这些服务器实际上是它们自己的操作系统。 在该模型中，应用程序服务器会贪婪地消耗所有可用的主机内存并连续运行数月，从而使较长的 JVM 启动阶段和繁重的初始资源占用成为完全可以接受的权衡。 但这种整体设计与现代 Web 应用程序的通常构建方式相冲突，尤其是在工程文化已转向微服务的快节奏启动环境中。 如今，您通常希望部署一组独立的容器，这些容器可以立即启动，并且可以动态水平扩展以应对突然的流量峰值。 仅仅为了运行一个轻量级的独立服务而在一个巨大的预热 Java 环境中运行直接违背了这一原则。

我一直想知道如何创建一个符合现代开发实践的 Clojure 运行时，而 Jolt 是我解决这个问题的尝试。 当然，已经有许多 Clo​​jure 方言了。 尤其是 Jank，我个人很高兴能够关注这一成果。 距离完整的 Clojure 实现已经很远了，但它的重点是与核心语言的兼容性。 不幸的是，大多数现有的 Clojure 库最终都依赖于与 JVM 主机的某些互操作，这意味着它们在不进行修改的情况下将无法在这些方言上运行。

如果能够利用现有的成熟且经过实战测试的库组成的生态系统，那就太好了，而且我很想知道在 Chez 运行时之上提供特定于 Java 的填充程序有多么可行。 我的主要认识是，大多数库实际上并没有使用太多 Java 标准库表面来进行互操作，一旦您映射了一些包，例如 java.io、java.time 和其他一些包，那么您就可以运行当前 Clojure 堆栈的大部分，而无需重新实现或将其移植到新平台。 同样值得注意的是其他努力，例如针对 Go 的放手方言，它正在遵循类似的计划。 主要区别在于我们的目标平台以及它们开放的生态系统。

今天有效的方法

如今，Jolt 支持许多流行的 Clojure 库。 使用 Ring、Reitit、Selmer 和 HoneySQL 构建成熟的 Ring 应用程序已经成为可能。 整个测试套件都通过的库的完整列表可以在官方文档网站上看到。 一些库（例如 Reitit）会下降到 Java，但这不是问题，因为 Jolt 可以使用 Jolt 库提供垫片。 例如，路由器库实现了Reitit所需的reitit.Trie Java类。 因此，如果您需要访问核心语言中没有可用垫片的特定 Clojure 库，您始终可以自己添加它们以使它们正常工作。 在许多情况下，这不需要从头开始完成，因为您可以利用成熟的Scheme 或C 库来实现底层功能，并且只需编写一些粘合代码即可将功能公开为Java API。

Jolt 使用 deps.edn 来管理依赖项和别名，其方式与常规 Clojure 基本相同。 作为奖励，您还可以指定本机 C 依赖项，如此处所示。 系统上必须提供这些库才能使用。

使用 Clojure 的主要乐趣始终是其交互式开发周期，因此完全支持熟悉的 nREPL 工作流程。 您可以启动 Jolt 应用程序，从您最喜欢的编辑器中插入，然后像在 JVM 上运行 Clojure 时一样开发它。 此外，nREPL 也可以嵌入到已编译的发行二进制文件中。

作为该项目的一部分，我想做的另一件事是找出 Clojure 的实际构成，作为要遵循的规范。 因此，我正在构建一致性规范、EBNF 和 RFC，同时添加对现有库的支持并在此过程中发现不同的怪癖。 我想对 Jank 团队的 clojure-test-suite 表示赞扬，它对于引导和相信核心语言语义（例如阅读器、特殊形式和大部分 clojure.core 是正确的）非常宝贵。 然而，它缺乏许多库所依赖的 JVM 主机契约，因此构建语料库的大部分努力都花在了缩小这一差距上。 它从参考 JVM Clojure 中获取每个预期值，目前保存了大约 3,500 个案例，每个案例都标记为可移植（：common，任何忠实的 Clojure 方言都必须满足的要求）或依赖于主机（：jvm，执行互操作）。 认证步骤会根据真实的 Clojure 重新评估整个语料库，以捕获未分类的差异，确保合约不会悄然发生变化。

引擎盖下

让图书馆正常工作暴露了一系列反复出现的问题。 在主机端，Jolt 镜像了流行的 Clojure 库常用的 9 个 java.* 包。 其中包括 java.io、java.lang、java.util、java.time、java.math、java.net、java.nio、java.sql 和 java.text，这些包中包含大约一千种方法和字段实现。 值得注意的是，Jolt 并未提供 JVM 标准库的完整重新实现。 虽然这些类中的大多数都带有完整的方法集，但有一些类仅解决了足够的问题？ 并进行类型检查以正确着陆，并且像 bean 和 proxy 这样的少数内容会被记录为部分内容。 在很多情况下，像 Math/sqrt、(StringBuilder.) 和实例这样的互操作形式就足够了？ 解决真实的行为，而不是需要探索出真正的类层次结构。

由于底层没有 JVM，类标识必须使用层次结构图来合成以支持实例？ 和（类 x），并且原始 Chez 运行时错误需要映射到可靠的 JVM 异常层次结构，以便 catch 分派可以正常工作。 简单的价值平等无法捕捉到的情况是这里最棘手的。 例如，整个 seq 生成器系列必须在构造上偷懒，在 32 元素运行中实现从左到右、记忆和分块，以匹配 Clojure 的功能，而数字塔则需要完整的 JVM 传染规则以及用于未经检查的数学的 64 位包装。 然而，真正的测试只是为各个库运行真正的测试套件，以发现微妙的实现怪癖和 Java 主机交互。 移植spec.alpha、core.logic、core.async、test.check、tools.reader、rewrite-clj和其他几十个解决了大多数边缘情况，例如返回代码形式的数据读取器、命名空间映射文字、*print-length*和*print-level*、跨deftype和reify合并的协议方法，甚至是通过Chez的弱对和监护人连接的具有真正GC驱逐的软引用和弱引用。

就占用空间而言，在没有优化的情况下，最小的二进制文件编译后约为 13 兆，直接链接时仅为 8 兆。 就实际性能而言，Jolt 目前在许多基准测试中与 JVM Clojure 相当，在大多数情况下都比 JVM Clojure 慢 2 倍左右，而在少数情况下仅比 JVM Clojure 差 7 倍左右。 这完全是通过成熟的 Chez 运行时实现的。 当然，就像我们可以使用常规 Clojure 降级到 Java 一样，Jolt 也可以让您在需要峰值性能的情况下使用 Scheme 甚至 C FFI 执行相同的操作。

为什么不等莱顿呢？

一旦 Leyden 项目落地，它将解决与 JVM 相关的臭名昭著的启动时间和内存占用问题，因此您可能会想知道是否还有必要使用专门的方言来解决这些问题。 我认为这是因为我们可以通过利用斯大林计划中看到的完整程序优化等技巧来进一步推动优化。 虽然莱顿和斯大林都依赖于形式化的封闭世界约束，但他们的最终目标却完全不同。 Leyden 主要关注将计算从运行时转移到构建时，以保证不会动态加载新类。 这种方法允许 AOT 编译器执行积极的死代码消除以及去虚拟化和单态化，因为了解完整的类层次结构允许虚拟方法调用变成直接跳转或内联代码。 但 Leyden 无法实现与斯大林相同类型的结构擦除，因为它必须尊重 Java 语义。 Leyden 避免了英雄式的流程分析，仍然受到有关 Java 对象布局和内存模型的严格规则的约束。 另一方面，斯大林式优化器在证明对象寿命较短时，可以完全自由地缩放对象或绕过标准垃圾收集路径。 与 Leyden 不同，它不受需要生成遵循 JVM 语义并与 Java 垃圾收集器集成的程序的限制。

在 Chez Scheme 之上构建还回避了 Java 根本无法摆脱的架构包袱，最明显的例子是适当的尾调用优化。 从定义上来说，Scheme 强制要求 TCO，因此将 Clojure 编译为 Scheme 可以免费提供无限的相互递归函数，并包含 JVM 积极反对的函数式编程范式。 内存模型和本机互操作性提供了另一个重要的差异，使 C 生态系统如此有吸引力。 即使 Leyden 缩小了 JVM 占用空间，每个值仍然承载着 Java 对象头的重量。 Chez Scheme 的运行开销要低得多，并且启动时间为微秒。 从Scheme中，您可以直接操作原始C指针和结构，包装高性能库，而无需JNI和Panama在托管边界产生序列化和上下文切换税。

还有一个现实是数值计算，JVM 强制将动态类型放入装箱对象中。 因此，除非您严重依赖原始类型提示，否则在紧密的数学循环期间会产生巨大的垃圾收集压力。 方案实现利用 NaN 装箱或标记指针等技术直接在 CPU 寄存器中表示动态类型，从而允许动态数学运算更接近于金属运行。 重要的是，使用 JVM 实现 Leyden 级别的性能依赖于使用提前编译阶段，该阶段破坏了交互式 REPL 体验，而正是这种体验使得 Clojure 如此强大。 Jolt 可以利用 Chez Scheme 生成高度优化的本机代码，同时保留动态绑定。 您可以获得紧密编译的二进制文件的执行配置文件，同时保持 Clojure 的交互式灵魂完好无损。 当然，当不需要交互功能时，您仍然可以执行 AOT 以获得更好的性能优势。

Jolt 还支持以 ClojureScript 编译器为模型的 Tree Shaking。 当包含库时，可以跟踪实际使用库的哪些部分。 跟踪调用图并消除在任何地方都没有引用的名称空间和函数，从而形成精益的发布二进制文件。

桌面和本机互操作

除了原始性能和内存占用之外，更大的长期好处还在于可以直接访问Scheme 和C 生态系统。 一个明显的例子是构建桌面应用程序。 目前，最常见的方法是使用 Java UI 框架的包装器并接受来自 Java 运行时的膨胀。 将 Clojure 引入 Chez Scheme 可以避免这个问题，让您可以通过外部函数接口直接绑定到 GTK 等本机 C 库。 尽管存在 Java 本机接口，甚至较新的巴拿马项目，但在跨越从托管内存到本机指针的边界时，它们必然会带来摩擦和开销。 从Scheme中，C边界几乎变得不可见，使得与基础本机库交互成为可能，而无需付出运行时代价或编写大量样板C代码来弥补差距。

为了证明这个概念，我制作了 glimmer，它使用 Reagent 风格的反应原子来驱动 GTK 组件。 一个经典的 TodoMVC 应用程序说明了如何与 Jolt 完全无缝地使用本机 UI 工具包。 反应式数据模型已证明自己是 ClojureScript 中极其有效的工具，并且使用 Reagent 风格 API 包装 GTK 将这些相同的人体工程学原理带入本机桌面环境，从而使我们两全其美。 您可以使用反应性原子和声明性组件结构，同时使用快速的本机工具包进行渲染。 该应用程序最终的内存占用量比 Java 虚拟机中运行的任何应用程序的内存占用量都要小得多。 当然，我们都知道并喜爱的 nREPL 驱动开发也适用于此。

我们甚至可以更进一步，使用 glimmer-gl 从 GTK 窗口内部创建 OpenGL 上下文。 您可以在此处了解反应式方法如何在这种情况下无缝工作。

我什至设法让整个 Quake 风格的 FPS 在 Jolt 上运行。 原生构建反应式 3D 场景通常涉及使用低级语言手动管理状态突变。 直接从 Clojure 驱动 OpenGL 管道可以轻松动态地对复杂的视觉状态进行建模，而无需牺牲原始性能。 您可以构建快速启动并平稳运行的本地图形密集型工具。 开发人员体验完全在 Clojure 的功能世界中，而繁重的工作则由本机库完成。

部署与架构

部署或分发 Jolt 应用程序仅意味着为目标平台构建二进制文件，用户不需要在其计算机上安装 JVM 运行时即可运行它。 事实上，您甚至不需要安装 Chez Scheme 来使用 Jolt 进行开发，因为它会生成自己的独立的 joltc 二进制文件。 另一个优点是可以创建一个可嵌入到 C、C++ 或 Rust 项目中的 Jolt 库，其中 Jolt 可以在高性能本机代码之上通过 nREPL 提供高级 Clojure API 和交互性。 这对于具有密集图形可视化的项目甚至使用 Godot 等引擎的游戏尤其有吸引力。

在架构上，我将编译器分为三个部分：语言本身有一个 Clojure-in-Clojure 编译器，然后是一个由主机层和 Java 互操作垫片组成的方案部分。 作为一个延伸目标，我希望能够从交易中获得一个可移植的 Clojure 实现，它可以轻松地针对不同的运行时，以应对不需要 JVM 互操作的情况。 您可以从一组最小不可约形式开始，然后像种子一样生长编译器本身，以便主机（无论是Scheme还是其他任何东西）只需要为该最小引导代码提供支持。 然而，将功能转移到 Clojure 层也可能与原始性能不一致。 这里的一个折衷方案可能是拥有一个可移植的方案层，它可以针对不同的实现。

虽然 Chez 为我们提供了 Clojure 的良好总体目标，但它做出了一些架构假设，例如能够将指令写入内存的一部分，然后执行该代码，这不适用于 WASM 等目标，因为 WASM 认为这种行为是安全问题。 将主机层与 Chez 解耦并使其轻松移植到不同的 Scheme 运行时将非常有用，这将使我们能够通过 Guile 访问 WASM 的交互式开发等内容。 由于主机代码已经与核心语言实现分离，因此需要进行更多重构，但隔离 Chez 特定位并在顶部构建通用方案层并不困难。

论法学硕士的建设

在这一点上，值得讨论的是这个项目是使用 LLM 工具构建的。 现实情况是，否则我根本不可能承担如此规模的项目。 手动编写完整的 Clojure 移植将是一个全职项目。 使用代码生成工具实际上使工作变得容易处理，并让我能够快速证明这个概念。 当然，自然会有与这种方法相关的担忧，以及当我不是自己编写每一行代码时如何确保代码质量。 所以，我想花一点时间在这里讨论这个问题。

我要提出的论点是，代码是否完全由手工编写或由法学硕士生成是错误的关注点。 要了解其中的原因，我们必须考虑软件开发实际上是如何大规模运作的。

有一种观点认为，手工编写的代码必须更加有意，几乎必须是根据定义，因为它需要维护者自己实际将其放入其中。 当然，这是事实，但一旦项目规模超过一定规模或有多个维护者，没有人真正掌握完整的代码。 因此，添加的任何新代码总是在理解有限的情况下完成的。 手写的代码不应该等同于忠实地表达了意图；如果是这样的话，那么我们就永远不会出现软件错误。 人类总是会犯错误，在 LLM 使用之前就不乏有错误的代码就清楚地证明了这一点。

我对多年来一直维护的项目中的大部分代码也不太熟悉。 我几个月前编写的任何代码也可能是其他人编写的。 当我需要进行更改时，我会通读代码并弄清楚它在做什么，并且我依靠测试工具来确保我不会引入回归。

对于人类来说，一次性将所有大型项目都记在脑子里是不可能的。 当您从事一个项目时，您会不断忘记并重新学习代码。 对于多人一起工作、没有人知道其他人在想什么的项目来说，情况更糟。 我们查看代码并尝试在头脑中建立足够的上下文以进行必要的更改。 当我们错误判断上下文或误解现有代码时，我们最终会犯错误。

我们判断项目是否真正可靠的方法是根据它们的规范和测试水平、开发人员的经验以及他们在野外看到的使用量。 所有这些相同的工具都可以很好地处理 LLM 生成的代码，就像处理手工编写的代码一样。

将设计决策外包给法学硕士而不审查输出或进行适当的测试几乎肯定会产生低质量的代码，但这与有人只是将一些代码拼凑在一起而不是真正思考问题没有什么不同。 与法学硕士合作并不意味着将你的思维外包给机器。 这些工具实际上所做的是自动化生成代码的机械方面。 一旦编写完成，您就可以阅读、理解并更改它，就像处理任何其他代码一样。

我的流程往往是为代理提供实现特定功能的重点任务，然后对每个功能进行提交，并检查功能是否已按我的预期实现。 这与常规代码审查过程并没有什么不同，众所周知，常规代码审查过程是捕获错误的好方法。

我对代码也非常有信心，因为它通过了 clojure-test-suite 以及许多现有 Clojure 库的测试。 我为其构建的测试语料库可确保当我进行更改时代码不会回归。 我设置的基准可确保性能不会下降。 所有这些事情加在一起给了我与我对相同范围的手写项目一样的信心。 我进一步认为，编译器是法学硕士特别好的目标，因为它们的定义很好地指定了。 你有数据进来，相应的数据也出来。 当您实现一种语言时，关于什么构成正确行为几乎没有什么歧义。

结论

最终，项目的质量取决于其维护者的技能和兴趣，而不是代码的生成方式。 如果某人已经编写代码多年，并且在交付高质量项目方面拥有良好的记录，那么应该假设，当他们在工作中使用 LLM 这样的新工具时，输出将与他们之前已经展示的标准一致。

如果您已经走到这一步，那么我希望您会发现这个项目很有趣，并且您会尝试一下 Jolt。 此时情况相当稳定，编译器二进制文件可通过 GitHub 版本和自制程序获得。 我在 Clojurians Slack 上建立了一个 #jolt 频道用于讨论和更新，如果您有兴趣合作或讨论该项目的方向，请顺便过来。 或者，如果您遇到任何问题或有建议，请随时在 GitHub 上提出问题或提出拉取请求。
